GA4 Certification Micro-Application Part 5

Author

Kennon Holton-Tran

Published

June 26, 2026

1 Overview

This report completes Part 5 of the GA4 Certification Micro-Applications. The purpose of this assignment is to document my Google Analytics Certification exam result, reflect on the areas that were most challenging, and connect what I learned to my Culminating Experience Project measurement plan.

2 Proof of Completion

Figure 1 shows my Google Analytics Certification completion evidence.

3 Exam Reflection

3.1 Results Summary

My result was Pass. I received a 100% score on the Google Analytics Certification exam.

3.2 Top Three Challenging Topics

The three topics that felt most challenging were:

  • Attribution models and attribution scope, especially understanding how credit is assigned differently across reports.
  • Key events and Google Ads conversion workflow, including when an event needs to be marked as key before it can be used for advertising.
  • GA4 integrations and data tools, including BigQuery export, Data Import, Measurement Protocol, and the different Analytics APIs.

3.3 Two Concepts I Would Redo

The first concept I would want to review again is the difference between User Acquisition and Traffic Acquisition reporting. User Acquisition focuses on how users first arrive, while Traffic Acquisition focuses more on session-level sources. This is important because choosing the wrong report could lead to the wrong conclusion about campaign performance.

The second concept I would review again is attribution reporting in the Advertising workspace. I understand that attribution models help explain how credit is distributed across different touchpoints, but this is still an area where the details matter. I would want to be extra careful when comparing attribution reports to standard acquisition reports.

3.4 CEP Connection

One thing I will change in my CEP measurement and analysis plan is to focus more clearly on a specific KPI instead of relying only on general traffic or engagement. For the Google demo readiness check, I selected purchase conversion rate because it connects user behavior to a meaningful business outcome. In my CEP, I would use the same idea by choosing a KPI that directly supports the project goal, then pairing it with a supporting report or exploration so the analysis leads to a clear next step.

4 GA4 Readiness Check

For the readiness check, I used the Google Analytics demo account.

4.1 KPI

The KPI I selected is purchase conversion rate.

4.2 Supporting Report

The supporting report I selected is the Ecommerce purchases report in the Google Merchandise Store demo property.

This KPI supports the project goal because it measures whether users are moving from product interest to actual purchase behavior. The Ecommerce purchases report helps identify which products and user actions are connected to stronger conversion outcomes. This makes the analysis more useful because it focuses on measurable results instead of only general site activity.

5 Final Reflection

Completing the Google Analytics Certification helped connect the separate GA4 topics into a fuller analytics workflow. The exam covered data collection, reporting, explorations, audiences, key events, integrations, and activation. The main takeaway for my CEP is that a good measurement plan should connect a clear KPI, a supporting report or exploration, and a specific action that could improve the outcome.
